title: Needle or Cannula: What Method Do I Prefer For Tear Trough？
filename: Needle or Cannula： What Method Do I Prefer For Tear Trough？.en.vtt
excerpt: Hi guys hope you had a good weekend I'm I've put myself under pressure today because I'm actually on the school run and I've really got to go and pick up both my sons in the next and four minutes if I've got enough time to go through one question which I had after the canula vide…

segments.text: hi guys hope you had a good weekend I'm I've put myself under pressure today because I'm actually on the school run and I've really got to go and pick up both my sons in the next and four minutes if I've got enough time to go through one question which I had after the canula video which was which is better for tear trough because I mentioned basically using both needle and cannula for doing tear trough and so the question is what's actually better which is a really great question because it's a complex answer and so that the benefit of cannula as we know is to reduce the chance in particularly the tear travels about reducing the chance of bruising and reducing the chance of bruising is a huge benefit when you're treating the tear trough because the social impact of having a bruise is quite significant because bruising around your eye is associated with lots of things that people don't want people to think of them so usually it's kind of I've been hit by someone or I've got too drunk and fallen over so we can't

underestimate what it means to be able to walk around with the bruise it makes them very unhappy and if you can reduce the chance of it we should definitely do that so that's firstly why cannula comes into the equation because you can reduce bruising now lots of people do worry about blindness as a risk with tear trough it's actually statistically not one of those treatments that's particularly highly associated with blindness and highest risk is treating your frown line or rubella noses are also a bigger risk and tear trough really isn't just because the blood vessel isn't actually the angular artery isn't that close if you do it in the normal way so you can treat tear trough without having that being like the number one risk but all patients seem to think that it is so cannula is often also why that conversation comes up so and why would you use a needle to treat a tear trough and when there's bruising well no not the main thing is with a cannula it's quite difficult to get deep so as a rule of thumb and something which I've always thought and then actually meridian Tamiya said it to me last time we when we're doing this my first lot of

training with him I always think of cannula as being in the fat and you can try and get really down deep and scrape along the parry Jim is pretty uncomfortable and it's difficult and you're probably still not quite as deep as you would be with a needle and therein lies the big difference which is if you're using a needle you can get on the periosteum that means you can hide this filler really deep underneath all the other tissue and the only thing it just sits on the bone with a fat on top of it as soon as you start using a cannula you're mixing filler and fat and I think in particular people that becomes a bit mobile you know it tracks a bit of moisture as the iron moves up and down it changes it can change the position a bit more easily and the biggest factor is moisture attraction there are only two fillers I think you should be using in the tear trough one is Juvederm Barbella and the other one is tear so reduce radensky too so both of those are well established for using the tear trough now people use all sorts of other things as well and you might be getting great results and that's fine and please

you know be interested to hear them but I think a bit of manufacturer of backing is quite a good idea when you're doing something like the tear trough so and so that's I recommend is well it's understanding that difference so if your issue this is another really subtle difference with tear troughs some people have a tear trough that is partly caused by actually the fat bulges out the ligament holds the tear trough and holds the tissue around there in place and it looks like a shadow but actually you've got a little bit of bulge from the fat and that's gonna be a lot harder to treat and you definitely should not be putting a cannula into those people because they are you much more likely to be adding to the problem of fat and you can in some people blend in that transition but I would be using a needle in those cases so that I'm really down deep elevating the ligament almost and trying not to involve any filler with fat and you start with time if you I always didn't fully was with needles for years never did anything set needles and then I started then I was

trying to candy and I thought this is amazing all new cannula and then I started to get people coming back with little bits of puffiness and I I've over time started to get better at predicting who those people are because I still prefer cannula I just don't do it on everyone and I think the rates are better but it's I'm looking for those kind of the slightly puffy lower lids I mean talking slight because very puffy lids are not going to do it anyway and slightly puffy a sense of a fullness rather than just emptiness and those people I'm airing towards let's hide this filler further away rather than if it's just a clearly lost volume and you can blend that in nicely with some Bella for Bella then it'll probably work quite well and quite safely so it's difficult to really pin down exactly you know in words because there's kind of an instinct you get after treating many people and seeing a few people come back and it is a well known side effect there's a little bit of puffiness after treatment and we're always in this battle to try and reduce that get that down to zero so that's my thought process look for true pure volume loss a

bit easier with a cannula to say yes is the right instrument and then anything with is it withers a sense that the fat might be moving quite a lot as their eyes are moving or a little bit of fullness you want to maybe towards a needle and you should get slightly fewer people coming back by far the biggest variable is product because I know because in the years gone by when I used to wouldn't first started doing tear trough and I was taught with fillers afore now I wouldn't dream of using so they basically attract moisture we were always taught to under treat and it would hopefully expand to just the right amount which I could do sometimes but it was kind of one in 25 people you'd have a little bit of a problem and it wasn't always media it could be six eight you know twelve months later and it's definitely the new products are way better so choose your product correctly and start to think about needle versus can you learn the ones who come back you will learn from the people who unfortunately do need a reversal or a massage and I'll do another talk on how to deal with it because you don't always have to reverse it and because I'm pretty sure my wife is going to come out that door any second and say we're late to pick up the boys she's in a

meeting and I think she's probably forgotten I'm a good knock on her door but I hope that helps and that's how I choose between needle and cannula and any other questions please loving the questions coming through you can comment on this thread you can inbox me you can email me at Timmy dr. Tim Pierce calm and I'll try and which we're going to try and do more of these and make them a bit better produced over time but the moment is just about being regular so pleased I managed to squeeze a scent before I go on the school and thanks for watching if anyone if you think anyone else might be interested in this if you tagged them on the post that'd be great or if you think is useful to other people you want to share it that'd be amazing so thanks very much for watching I'll catch you again probably tomorrow it will be tomorrow we'll do another one thanks guys
